title: Coconut bread
subtitle: Caribbean sweet coconut loaf
excerpt: A slightly sweet, tender loaf with desiccated coconut and coconut milk worked into an enriched white bread dough. The coconut flavour is present but not aggressive, a gentle sweetness and fragrance that makes the bread distinctive without being a dessert. Caribbean coconut bread is eaten toasted for breakfast, with butter, at tea time, and alongside savoury dishes where its slight sweetness provides a contrast.
sourceNotes: Coconut bread appears across the Caribbean in various forms, in Trinidad, Jamaica, and Barbados, each with minor variations in sweetness and coconut intensity. The use of coconut milk in place of dairy milk produces a slightly denser but more flavourful loaf. The bread is most strongly associated with Barbados and the Eastern Caribbean, where it is sold from bakeries alongside coconut bake and salt bread.

content.text: The coconut milk replaces dairy milk in the dough. Use full-fat tinned coconut milk for the best flavour and texture. Warm it slightly before adding to the yeast, yeast needs warmth (around 35°C) to activate, and cold coconut milk straight from the tin will slow the prove considerably.

The desiccated coconut adds texture. Toast it briefly in a dry pan before adding to the dough, toasted coconut has a much more pronounced flavour than raw desiccated coconut and the difference in the finished bread is significant.

Combine the {{plain-flour}}, {{yeast-dried}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a bowl. Add the warm {{coconut-milk}}, {{vegetable-oil}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and 50 ml of warm water. Mix to a soft dough. Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Knead in the toasted {{coconut-desiccated}} evenly. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and prove for 1 hour until doubled in size. Knock back, shape into a log, and place in a lightly oiled 900 g loaf tin. Cover and prove for 45 minutes to 1 hour until risen to the tin rim. Heat the oven to 190°C. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until golden and hollow-sounding when tapped. Cool on a wire rack.

items.fix: Warm the coconut milk to body temperature before adding. Cold liquid prevents the yeast from activating. Check yeast freshness with warm water and a pinch of sugar. It should foam within 10 minutes
items.cause: Yeast inhibited by cold coconut milk or old yeast

items.fix: Toast the desiccated coconut in a dry pan until lightly golden. Toasted coconut has three to four times the flavour intensity of raw
Cover loosely with foil after 20 minutes if the top is browning too fast. The internal temperature should reach 90°C when done
items.symptom: Loaf too dark on top before 40 minutes

content.text: Coconut bread is the sweet bread of the Caribbean bakery. In Barbados it sits alongside salt bread, coconut bake, and currant rolls. It is a breakfast or tea-time bread, eaten toasted with butter, eaten with fish cakes, eaten as a snack. The flavour sits between a slightly sweet bread and a cake: it is definitely bread, but with a sweetness and fragrance that marks it as specifically Caribbean. In Britain it appears in Caribbean bakeries and is baked at home by the diaspora as a weekend bread.
